title: ABC9 crashes on executing ABC9_OPS pass
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

I'm trying to synthesize the [VTR benchmark](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/tree/master/vtr_flow/benchmarks/verilog). I've added all missing ram definitions in these files here:

[vtr_benchmark_files.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/8932912/vtr_benchmark_files.zip)

I'm starting the synthesis like this:
```
yosys-abc9 -l yosys.log boundtop.ys
```

boundtop.ys:
``` 
read_verilog -nomem2reg -yydebug boundtop.v
synth_xilinx -dff -flatten -noiopad -edif ch_intrinsics.edif
write_verilog -noexpr -norename boundtop_syn.v
```

Yosys Version: Yosys 0.17+76 (git sha1 d9bb10ba5, gcc 4.8.5 -fPIC -Os)
My target platform is xc7a100tcsg324-1. 

## Expected behavior

Yosys-ABC9 should finish synthesizeing the design, like Vivado and Yosys (non-abc9).
The benchmakrs for which Yosys-ABC9 crashes are:

boundtop.v
mkDelayWorker32B.v
or1200.v
spree.v

## Actual behavior

Yosys-ABC9 crashes with the following message:
```
...
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.

2.44.16. Executing ABC9_OPS pass (helper functions for ABC9).
terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::out_of_range'
  what():  vector::_M_range_check
Aborted (core dumped)
```

Full log included in .zip file [vtr_benchmark_files.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/8932912/vtr_benchmark_files.zip).
code_blocks:
read_verilog -nomem2reg -yydebug boundtop.v
synth_xilinx -dff -flatten -noiopad -edif ch_intrinsics.edif
write_verilog -noexpr -norename boundtop_syn.v

...
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.

2.44.16. Executing ABC9_OPS pass (helper functions for ABC9).
terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::out_of_range'
  what():  vector::_M_range_check
Aborted (core dumped)
